metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a plan-body rationale section (or expand 'Why') that explains: (a) why the code-review gate uses a per-citation region gate rather than plan-review's whole-artifact floor (e.g. code diffs are citation-scoped by nature — a region is meaningful while a 'whole artifact' floor makes no sense for a diff), (b) what 'dso-style arbiter/relation-taxonomy/oscillation-loop' means in this context and why the lightweight floor is sufficient (i.e. what problem the arbiter would solve that the floor already handles), so the ADR author has grounded rationale to record rather than having to reconstruct it.
findings.finding: The plan's 'Why' section motivates writing the ADR but supplies no positive rationale for the specific divergences from plan-review (per-citation region gate vs whole-artifact floor; typed local/Gerrit keyspaces; content-addressed resilient region detection). AC2 explicitly requires the ADR to record 'why the lightweight novelty floor suffices' over a dso-style arbiter — yet the plan never explains what a dso-style arbiter is in this context or why the floor beats it. An implementer writing this ADR must reconstruct both the divergence rationale and the arbiter-rejection rationale independently, producing exactly the under-specified design record the plan is trying to prevent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `region_gated_floor` tri-state value vocabulary (e.g., `CHANGED | UNCHANGED | UNKNOWN`) and specify what `UNKNOWN==raise` means operationally (exception, gate veto, or advisory escalation) before or within the ADR, so the ADR documents a concrete, implementable contract rather than an unnamed three-outcome black box.
findings.finding: `region_gated_floor` is referenced as a named architectural conjunct in the plan's What section and AC1, but has zero existence in the codebase (no matches in src/). The plan assigns it a tri-state with a specific fallback value ('UNKNOWN==raise') but never defines the tri-state's value vocabulary, what 'raise' means operationally (exception? gate veto? advisory?), or the decision protocol for when each state is returned.
findings.location: What section ('the new region_gated_floor conjunct and its tri-state fail-safe (UNKNOWN==raise)'); AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Decision protocol for `region_gated_floor`' subsection to the What section specifying: (1) the three outcome values and their semantics; (2) the trigger condition for UNKNOWN (e.g., region hash lookup fails, no prior sidecar, rebase detected); (3) whether UNKNOWN causes an exception, a gate-level veto, or falls through to the normal floor.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify what 'typed session/change keyspaces' means concretely — i.e., the data type (NamedTuple, Literal enum, or tagged union) and its field vocabulary — and describe the enforcement mechanism that prevents a local session key from seeding a Gerrit change key, so the ADR documents an implementable contract rather than a bare naming intention.
findings.finding: The plan names 'typed session/change keyspaces' as a key ADR topic ('typed session/change keyspaces and why local must not seed a change's first Gerrit review'), but the code-review package has no typed keyspace definition for novelty keying — `change_id` exists as a plain string field in `sidecar.py`, and there is no session keyspace definition in `code_review/`. The plan gives no vocabulary for what makes these keyspaces 'typed' vs the bare `change_id` string.
findings.location: What section ('typed session/change keyspaces and why local must not seed a change's first Gerrit review')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define what a 'region' means in the code-review novelty context (e.g., a (file, hunk-hash) tuple derived from the diff), specify the hash basis that makes it content-addressed, and state the invariant that makes it rebase-resilient — before writing the ADR, so the documented design is concrete rather than aspirational.
findings.finding: The plan names 'content-addressed region detection and its resilience to rebase/force-push' as an ADR topic, but no code exists for region detection in the code-review context (content-addressed architecture exists only for snapshot caching). The plan gives no definition of what a 'region' is (file-level hunk? line range? citation path?), how it is content-addressed, or what invariant makes it resilient to rebase.
findings.location: What section ('content-addressed region detection and its resilience to rebase/force-push')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'surfaced-only prior set' explicitly — specify which prior findings are included (e.g., only blocking + advisory, excluding previously dropped) and why this subset is chosen over the full prior set — so the ADR can state a concrete, verifiable definition.
findings.finding: The plan's What section references 'surfaced-only prior set' as a named ADR concept but the term appears nowhere in the codebase or existing docs, and the plan gives no definition of what this set contains, how it is constructed, or how it differs from the full prior findings set.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief description in the Why or What section of what 'dso-style arbiter / relation-taxonomy / oscillation-loop' refers to and why it was considered-then-rejected, so AC2 is anchored to described plan work.
findings.finding: AC2 requires the ADR to record 'the explicit decision that NO dso-style arbiter/relation-taxonomy/oscillation-loop is added, and why the lightweight novelty floor suffices' — but neither the Why section nor the What section introduces or describes DSO-style arbitration, relation-taxonomy, or an oscillation-loop as a concept being considered or rejected. This AC has no anchoring described work in the plan body, making it an orphan criterion an implementer cannot verify from the plan alone.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Why section, add one sentence such as: 'An explicit non-goal is adding a DSO-style arbiter (a shared arbitration component with a relation-taxonomy and oscillation-detection loop used in other review contexts); the ADR must record why the lightweight novelty floor is sufficient and this heavier mechanism is out of scope.' Then add a corresponding bullet in the What ADR-content list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the exact file path of the 'code-review gate doc' in the Scope section (e.g. `docs/gates/code-review.md`) so an executor can find it without searching.
findings.finding: The plan references 'code-review gate doc' as a target file to update but never specifies its path. An executor must search or guess, risking updating the wrong file or creating a new one when an existing one should be amended.
findings.location: Scope section: 'code-review gate doc'; AC line 3: 'The code-review gate doc documents the novelty convergence behavior'
findings.suggested_fix: Add the concrete path to the Scope bullet: e.g. `docs/gates/code-review.md` (or wherever it lives), so the line reads: "docs/adr/, docs/gates/code-review.md, docs/reuse-surface.md".

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete file path for the 'code-review gate doc' in the plan (e.g., `docs/gates/code-review.md`) so implementers update the correct existing file rather than creating a duplicate or missing the target.
findings.finding: The 'code-review gate doc' referenced in Scope, What, and AC3 is never given a concrete file path. A future implementer cannot tell whether to update an existing file or create a new one, risking creation of a duplicate file or leaving the correct existing doc un-updated (stranded reference).
findings.location: Scope section ('code-review gate doc'); AC3 ('The code-review gate doc documents...')
findings.suggested_fix: Add the full relative path to the code-review gate doc in the Scope and What sections, e.g. `docs/gates/code-review.md` (or whatever the actual path is), matching the pattern already used for `docs/adr/` and `docs/reuse-surface.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify the specific file that is the 'code-review gate doc' (e.g., `docs/review-kernel.md`, or a new `docs/code-review-gate.md` to be created) and name it explicitly in the plan's What/Scope sections.
findings.finding: The plan's 'What' section says 'Update docs (code-review gate doc + docs/reuse-surface.md)' — asserting a named 'code-review gate doc' exists to be updated. No such file exists in `docs/`; the closest file is `docs/review-kernel.md` (which has a code-review consumer section) plus scattered ADRs. Without a concrete target file the update step is unverifiable.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'code-review gate doc' with the actual filename (e.g., 'docs/review-kernel.md § Code-review consumer section') or declare that a new `docs/code-review-gate.md` will be created as part of this plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve 'code-review gate doc' to a concrete file path (either an existing file such as `docs/review-kernel.md`, or declare a new file to be created such as `docs/code-review-gate.md`) so the edit target is unambiguous for implementers and AC3 reviewers.
findings.finding: The plan references 'code-review gate doc' as an edit target in the Scope, What, and AC3 sections but never resolves this to a concrete file path. No file named `docs/code-review-gate.md` (or any equivalent dedicated code-review gate doc) exists in the repository.
findings.location: Scope section: 'docs/adr/, code-review gate doc, docs/reuse-surface.md'; What section: 'Update docs (code-review gate doc + docs/reuse-surface.md)'; AC3: 'The code-review gate doc documents the novelty convergence behavior'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact file path for the 'code-review gate doc' update (e.g., clarify whether this means adding a section to `docs/review-kernel.md`, creating `docs/code-review-gate.md`, or updating another named file) so an executor has an unambiguous target.
findings.finding: The plan's What section says to update 'the code-review gate doc' but no standalone code-review gate doc exists; code-review gate documentation lives inside the shared `docs/review-kernel.md`. An executor has no specified target path and must guess or create a file.
findings.location:
What section ('Update docs (code-review gate doc + docs/reuse-surface.md)');
Scope section ('code-review gate doc')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'code-review gate doc' with the explicit path `docs/review-kernel.md` (the existing code-review consumer section, lines 61-94) or create and name a new `docs/code-review-gate.md` and update all cross-references.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the code-review novelty sub-call + rising_floor_drop is (a) already implemented (cite the file/function) or (b) a planned design — and adjust the ADR's framing accordingly (e.g., 'Decision: design adopted; implementation deferred to ticket X').
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'reuse of the novelty sub-call + rising_floor_drop' as the ADR's subject for the code-review gate, implying this reuse is an existing, implemented design to document — but `src/rebar/llm/code_review/` contains no calls to `score_novelty`, `rising_floor_drop`, or any novelty-floor orchestration. The code-review gate (`workflow_ops.py`, `batch_runner.py`, `shim.py`) does not currently invoke these kernel primitives.
findings.location: ## Why / ## What — 'reuse of the novelty sub-call + rising_floor_drop'
findings.suggested_fix: If the mechanism is not yet implemented, add a 'Status: Proposed' or 'Status: Partially implemented (kernel only; code-review orchestration pending)' note to the ADR, and reference the implementing ticket. If it is implemented, cite the specific code-review call-site in the ADR text.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete proving command per AC (e.g. `grep -q 'UNKNOWN.*raise' docs/adr/00*-code-review-novelty*.md` for AC1, `grep -q 'region_gated_floor' <gate-doc-path>` for AC3, and a link-validity check for AC4) so done-ness is verifiable from the repo.
findings.finding: None of the four acceptance criteria include a concrete proving command or check that produces evidence of completion. For a documentation plan, minimal but specific checks (e.g. grep for a key term, confirm cross-link target exists) are needed to distinguish 'file written' from 'criterion actually satisfied'.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a 'Verification' subsection with commands such as:
```
# AC1/AC2
grep -qE 'region_gated_floor|UNKNOWN.*raise|no.*arbiter' docs/adr/*-code-review-novelty*.md
# AC3
grep -qE 'carryover|region.gated|resilience' <code-review-gate-doc-path>
# AC4
grep -qE 'shared.*kernel|reuse-surface' docs/adr/*-code-review-novelty*.md && grep -qE 'code.review.*gate' docs/reuse-surface.md
```

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: Destructive/irreversible operation(s) with no stated safeguard.
findings.suggested_fix: State the safeguard (backup, dry-run, reversible migration, rollback) or justify the irreversibility explicitly (T4).

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `tests/unit/test_reuse_surface_doc.py` as a consumer of `docs/reuse-surface.md` in the plan and confirm whether the planned doc additions (code-review seam cross-links) require extending the anti-drift test.
findings.finding: `tests/unit/test_reuse_surface_doc.py` is a CI anti-drift gate that introspects every callable documented in `docs/reuse-surface.md` and asserts signatures match. The plan lists `docs/reuse-surface.md` as an edit target but does not enumerate this test as a consumer that must be checked after updates.
findings.location: What section: 'Update docs (code-review gate doc + docs/reuse-surface.md)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add or extend an AC to explicitly verify that the ADR documents the file-level v1 scope with line-level granularity deferred as a deliberate decision (e.g., extend AC1 or add AC5).
findings.finding: 'file-level v1 with line-level deferred' is listed in the What section as required ADR content (an explicit design decision to document) but no Acceptance Criterion verifies that this decision appears in the final ADR.
findings.location: What section (ADR content bullet list); Acceptance Criteria (all four ACs)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1: '…including the deliberate divergence from plan-review's whole-artifact floor, and the file-level v1 scope with line-level granularity explicitly deferred.' Or add a standalone AC5: 'The ADR records the file-level v1 scoping decision and the explicit deferral of line-level region detection with rationale.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the ADR number placeholder ('00NN') by specifying the numbering convention or the next available number, and add a step to register the new ADR in any existing ADR index/table of contents.
findings.finding: The ADR filename uses a placeholder number ('00NN') with no instruction on how to resolve it (e.g., 'pick next available number' or 'check docs/adr/README.md'). If no ADR index or numbering convention is referenced, the doc may be assigned a conflicting number or not registered in an existing index, making it non-navigable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC with a concrete validation step — e.g., 'following the cross-link from the code-review gate doc lands on the ADR section covering the tri-state fail-safe' or 'a new-contributor review confirms the ADR is self-contained without prior context'.
findings.finding: None of the four Acceptance Criteria carries a concrete validation mechanism — each is a content-presence check ('An ADR captures…', 'The ADR records…', 'doc documents…', 'docs cross-link…') with no before/after workflow comparison, operational signal, or dogfooding step.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC 4: 'Verify by following every cross-link from the gate doc and reuse-surface.md to confirm they resolve to the correct ADR section; confirm the ADR section headings map 1-to-1 to the design decisions listed in the What bullet.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `00NN` ADR number placeholder to a concrete filename (e.g. `0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md`) accounting for the highest existing ADR number in `docs/adr/`.
findings.finding: The ADR filename `docs/adr/00NN-code-review-novelty-convergence.md` uses an unresolved placeholder (`00NN`). The existing ADR directory contains files numbered up to 0036 (with some dual-numbered series). An implementer cannot determine the concrete filename from the plan alone, risking a number collision or non-sequential gap.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `00NN` with the next available number (currently `0037` based on the highest observed ADR number `0036`) and write the plan target as `docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or expand AC1/AC2) requiring the ADR to record the prior-art comparison for the two novel sub-mechanisms — content-addressed region detection (vs. git patch-id or line-anchor approaches) and typed local/Gerrit keyspaces (vs. a unified session with context tagging) — and why each was preferred.
findings.finding: The plan commits to two novel architectural patterns — (1) content-addressed region detection claimed to be resilient to rebase/force-push, and (2) typed local/Gerrit keyspaces with a seeding prohibition — without requiring the ADR to justify these choices against established alternatives (e.g., git patch-id for rebase-stable region identity, blame/diff-anchoring for region detection, a unified session keyspace with a context tag instead of distinct typed keyspaces). AC2 covers only the dso/arbiter rejection; no AC requires the author to examine or record why these specific novel sub-mechanisms were chosen over established diff-annotation or session-management approaches.
findings.location: ## What (content-addressed region detection; typed session/change keyspaces) + AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1 or as a new AC: 'The ADR records the alternatives-considered rationale for (a) content-addressed region detection over git patch-id / diff-line-anchor approaches and why it is resilient to rebase/force-push, and (b) typed local/Gerrit keyspaces over a unified session with a context tag, including the seeding-prohibition invariant and why a unified keyspace would not enforce it.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
